content.problemStatement:
A string SSS is called good if none of the substrings of SSS of length ≥2\ge 2≥2 are palindromic.

For e.g. S=S =S= hello\texttt{hello}hello is not good but S=S =S= world\texttt{world}world is good.

You are given a string AAA of length NNN (containing lowercase Latin letters only). Rearrange AAA to form a good string or determine it is not possible to do so.

Recall that a string is called palindromic if it reads the same backwards and forwards. For example, noon\texttt{noon}noon and level\texttt{level}level are palindromic strings.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the length of the string AAA.
The second line of each test case contains the string AAA of length NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, if it is possible to rearrange AAA to convert it into a good string, print `YES` in the first line and print the rearranged string in the second line. Otherwise, print `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for e.g. `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).


If there are multiple rearrangements of the string which are good, **print any of them**.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
A consists only of lowercase English letters.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case:
- a line with integer N, the length of the string.
- a line with a string A of length N consisting of lowercase Latin letters.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output either:
- "YES" on one line followed by a rearranged good string on the next line, or
- "NO" on a single line if no good rearrangement exists.
The string is good if none of its substrings of length at least 2 is a palindrome (equivalently, it contains no equal adjacent characters and no pattern x y x).

sampleTestCases.output:
YES
world
YES
lehol
YES
abcabca
NO
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: world\texttt{world}world is already a good string.
Test case 222: hello\texttt{hello}hello can be rearranged to form lehol\texttt{lehol}lehol which is good.
Test case 333: aaabbcc\texttt{aaabbcc}aaabbcc can be rearranged to form abcabca\texttt{abcabca}abcabca which is good.
Test case 444: It can be proven that none of the rearrangements of pppppr\texttt{pppppr}pppppr is good.

solution.approach:
A palindrome of length ≥2 always contains a palindrome of length 2 (aa) or 3 (aba). Therefore a string is good iff it has no two equal characters at distance 1 or 2. This means that any two equal letters must be placed at least three positions apart. Consequently the maximum frequency of any character must satisfy maxCount ≤ ceil(N/3). If this condition is violated, answer is NO.

If the condition holds we can construct a good string greedily:
1. Count frequencies of all letters and sort them in descending order.
2. Prepare the order of positions where we will place characters: first all indices 0,3,6,… then 1,4,7,… then 2,5,8,… (i.e. positions modulo 3 in the order 0,1,2). Consecutive positions in this list differ by at least 3 in the original string.
3. Walk through the sorted characters and fill the prepared positions one by one. Because the same character is always placed on consecutive slots of the position list, the distance between two equal letters is at least 3, guaranteeing the string is good.
The construction runs in O(N + alphabet·log alphabet) per test case, which is O(N) because the alphabet size is constant (26).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (total O(ΣN) over all test cases).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the resulting string and auxiliary arrays.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
from collections import Counter

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        s = next(it)
        freq = Counter(s)
        maxc = max(freq.values())
        if maxc > (N + 2) // 3:
            out_lines.append('NO')
            continue
        # sort characters by decreasing frequency
        items = sorted(freq.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])
        # prepare position order 0,3,6,...,1,4,7,...,2,5,8,...
        pos = []
        for start in range(3):
            for i in range(start, N, 3):
                pos.append(i)
        ans = [''] * N
        p = 0
        for ch, cnt in items:
            for _ in range(cnt):
                ans[pos[p]] = ch
                p += 1
        out_lines.append('YES')
        out_lines.append(''.join(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
